pattern: A two-track running-max DP where switching drinks costs a lookback of two hours instead of one models the mandatory cleanse hour.
hints: If you stay on the same drink, this hour's total is just last hour's total on that same drink plus this hour's boost - like a normal running sum.

If you just switched into a drink, the hour you actually gained a boost from that drink chain must have skipped the hour right before it, so it should connect back to the OTHER drink's total from two hours ago, not one.
Keep dpA[i] and dpB[i] as the best total ending with drink A (or B) fully consumed at hour i: dpA[i] = A[i] + max(dpA[i-1], dpB[i-2] or 0), symmetric for dpB; answer is max(dpA[n-1], dpB[n-1]).

edge_inputs.description: alternating which drink is momentarily better, tempting a naive greedy switch every hour
two consecutive switch opportunities where only one is worth taking

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(3, size)
    a = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    b = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [a, b]
